name: Density Intent
summary: Protocol defining spatial relationships, touch targets, and information quantity.

level.description: The spatial density profile controlling touch targets, spacing, and information quantity

metrics.description: Quantified, machine-measurable ranges per level — the contract the #1698 density-reality oracle divergence-checks against. The DENSITY signal is interElementGap (the spacing/whitespace ratio); touchTargetFloor is the separate a11y lane (measured absolutely by axe/WCAG 2.5.5/2.5.8, NOT the density metric — refuted as such in #1791) and is carried here only to keep the level vocabulary complete.

interElementGap.description: Typical spacing (px) between sibling content elements at this level — list rows, stacked form controls, card-grid items. The oracle measures rendered inter-element gaps and flags a page whose median falls outside its declared level's band. Bands are contiguous and bounded so both 'too tight' and 'too sparse' diverge.

touchTargetFloor.description: Minimum interactive hit-box size (px) per level. A11y lane, not the density metric (#1791). comfortable maps to WCAG 2.5.5 AAA (44px), wafer to 2.5.8 AA (24px).

description:
Spatial Contract

  • comfortable: 44px+ touch targets, 16–32px inter-element gaps. Whitespace is used for grouping (Consumer standard).
  • compact: 32px targets, 8–16px gaps. High information density, border-based grouping (Enterprise standard).
  • wafer: 24px targets, 2–8px gaps. Maximum data visibility for complex tooling/IDE interfaces.

Quantified bands live in metrics — the density-reality oracle (#1698) measures the rendered inter-element spacing/whitespace ratio against the declared level's interElementGap band.
